title: lewisia rediviva subsp. minor
intro: Lewisia rediviva subsp. minor, also known as the minor Bitterroot, is a subspecies of the Bitterroot plant, a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of iridoid glycosides and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Lewisia rediviva subsp. minor include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and skin conditions, while also exhibiting antioxidant and antibacterial activities. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
